messages.content: I live in an apartment, situated close to Chitagar Lake, with eye-catching view of the lake and plenty of sunlight during the day.  is there any mistakes?
I live in an apartment situated close to Chitgar Lake, with an eye-catching view of the lake and plenty of sunlight during the day.
does it need any improvement?

Your sentence is grammatically correct and conveys the intended message effectively. There isn't any significant improvement needed. However, if you want to make it even more polished, you can consider adding a few descriptive words:

"I live in a beautiful apartment nestled close to Chitgar Lake, boasting an eye-catching view of the shimmering waters and basking in an abundance of sunlight throughout the day."
I live in a beautiful apartment situated close to Chitgar Lake, with an eye-catching view of the water and plenty of sunlight during the day.

Your revised sentence sounds great! It effectively incorporates the suggested improvements by adding the word "beautiful" to enhance the description of the apartment and changing "lake" to "water" for a more specific reference to the view. Overall, it gives a vivid picture of your living situation near Chitgar Lake. Well done!

I used to dance professionally, a few years ago. It was folkloric azari dance. Currently, my main focus is on learning English. As a result, I do not have enough time to spend on my favorite hobby. is there any mistakes?
I used to dance professionally a few years ago. It was folkloric Azerbaijani dance. Currently, my main focus is on learning English, so I do not have enough time to spend on my favorite hobby.
I used to do Pilates for fitness purpose a few years ago. Currently, my main focus is learning English. As a result, I do not have enough time to spend on my favorite sport.

Your revised sentence is grammatically correct and effectively conveys your message. It clearly states that you used to do Pilates as a fitness activity in the past, but now your main focus is on learning English, which limits the time you have available for your favorite sport. Overall, it looks great!

I used to do Pilates for fitness purpose a few years ago. But, currently, my main focus is on learning English. As a result, I do not have enough time to spend on my favorite sport and physical activity.  check it again

Your revised sentence is grammatically correct and effectively conveys your message. It adds the conjunction "But" to indicate a contrast between your past fitness activity and your current focus on learning English. Additionally, it specifies that you don't have enough time for both your favorite sport and physical activity. Overall, it reads well and effectively communicates your point. Great job!

I have a brother and a sister. When we were kids, we did not have sibling rivalry. Because we had stable upbringing and both my parents played an active role in our school lives and home lives.  check the mistakes pls
I have a brother and a sister. When we were kids, we did not have sibling rivalry because we had a stable upbringing, and both my parents played an active role in our school and home lives.

Well, to be honest I have never thought about that, but if I were given a chance, I would go to Africa. I have always been curious about wildlife in Africa. I guess I would be fascinating to go on a safari and observe the wild creatures such as lions and elephants directly.  is there any mistakes?

Well, to be honest, I have never thought about that, but if I were given a chance, I would go to Africa. I have always been curious about the wildlife in Africa. I guess it would be fascinating to go on a safari and observe wild creatures such as lions and elephants directly.
The staff at Sweet Delights are friendly and welcoming. They are always happy to help customers choose the perfect pastry or bread to suit their tastes. is the grammar ok? should i say are or is?

The grammar in your sentence is correct. Since "staff" is a plural noun, you should use "are" to match the subject. Therefore, your sentence is grammatically accurate as stated:

"The staff at Sweet Delights are friendly and welcoming. They are always happy to help customers choose the perfect pastry or bread to suit their tastes."

I recently had the pleasure of hearing an interesting story that captivated my imagination. It was a tale of adventure and romance set in olden times, and the person who told me the story was my sister. Her descriptions of the landscapes and events were so detailed that I could see them in my mind's eye.

Your sentence is grammatically correct and effectively conveys your message. It beautifully describes your experience of hearing an interesting story from your sister, providing a sense of the tale being filled with adventure, romance, and captivating imagery. Well done!

I recently had the pleasure of hearing an interesting story that captivated my imagination. It was a tale of adventure and romance set in olden times, and the person who told me the story was my sister. Her descriptions of the landscapes and events were so detailed that I could see them in my mind's eye. As I listened, I was transported to another world, a place of wonder and magic that filled me with delight.
The story centers around a young woman named Emma who dreams of adventure and escapes from her boring life in a small village. One day, a stranger comes into town, and Emma is immediately drawn to him, sensing that he holds the key to the excitement and adventure. As Emma and the stranger go on abroad together, they encounter many obstacles and challenges. Along the way, they fall deeply in love, but their happiness is threatened by the forces that seek to keep them apart. is it better to describe the story in past tense or it is good now?
As the story came to a close, I felt a sense of sadness that the journey was over, but also a profound sense of satisfaction and completion.

The choice of using past tense or present tense when describing the story is subjective and depends on the context and narrative style you wish to convey. Both can be appropriate, but given the overall narrative style and the fact that you mentioned hearing the story in the past, it would be consistent to use past tense for the description of the story. 

Incorporating past tense in your description might look like this:

"As I listened, I was transported to another world, a place of wonder and magic that filled me with delight. The story centered around a young woman named Emma who dreamed of adventure and escape from her boring life in a small village. One day, a stranger came into town, and Emma was immediately drawn to him, sensing that he held the key to excitement and adventure. As Emma and the stranger went abroad, they encountered many obstacles and challenges. Along the way, they fell deeply in love, but their happiness was threatened by the forces that sought to keep them apart. As the story came to a close, I felt a sense of sadness that the journey was over, but also a profound sense of satisfaction and completion."

By using past tense consistently throughout the description, it maintains a consistent narrative style.
she tries to inspire her students to be more engaged in protecting and conserving the environment.
She tries to inspire her students to become more engaged in protecting and conserving the environment.
